skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI video workflows with inference.sh CLI guidance for text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, upscaling, foley, and video merging.
skill.summary: Create AI video command workflows for inference.sh models and utilities.
skill.author: tool-belt

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk because Markdown command examples, code spans, and sample URLs matched broad dangerous-pattern rules. Review found no prompt injection, executable Ruby code, weak cryptography, or credential exfiltration code. The skill is publishable with warnings because it intentionally guides users to run infsh commands and send prompts or media URLs to remote services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Through infsh
medium_findings.description: The skill provides many Bash examples that run infsh app commands. This is intended behavior, but users should approve commands and avoid untrusted shell interpolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible Markdown examples and match the skill purpose. The risk is real because running them invokes a local CLI with user-provided media prompts.

medium_findings.title: Remote Prompt and Media Processing
medium_findings.description: The examples send prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, and video URLs to inference.sh model apps. Private or licensed media could leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced inputs are explicit URL fields passed to remote generation apps. This is legitimate for the skill, but it has clear privacy and data handling impact.

medium_findings.title: Related Skill Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes npx skills add commands for related community skills. Users who copy them may install additional code outside this skill review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documentation examples and are not automatically executed. Supply-chain risk remains because they install additional skills from a community source.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown code fences and inline code spans, not Ruby backtick execution. No Ruby code is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown examples and tables. They do not define or execute Ruby backtick expressions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography alerts on lines 3 and 63 are prose and a Markdown table header. No cryptographic algorithm or hash use appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is the YAML description, and line 63 is a table heading. There is no code path using MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithms.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Placeholder URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are documentation links, an image preview, and placeholder media URLs. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or tracking logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are readable Markdown links or sample input values. They support the documented workflow and do not include secret collection code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands With User-Editable Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples place prompts and media references inside command-line input values. Users should escape quotes and review generated commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is potentially risky when users paste untrusted text into shell commands. The documentation itself does not show malicious input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Backed Video Generation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow depends on remote AI services for generation, upscaling, lipsync, foley, and merging. Users should avoid submitting confidential media.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly uses inference.sh model apps. Remote processing is central to the feature and creates predictable privacy and cost risks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Community Skill Installation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The related skills section documents commands that install more skills. Each referenced skill should be reviewed separately before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are not executed by this file, but they can expand the trusted code surface if users copy them.

content.value_statement: AI video tools use many model-specific inputs, which makes workflow setup slow and error-prone. This skill provides practical inference.sh command patterns for video generation and media post-processing.
actual_capabilities: Shows inference.sh commands for text-to-video generation with multiple models.
Provides image-to-video examples for animating still images.
Documents avatar and lipsync workflows with image and audio inputs.
Covers video upscaling, foley sound, and media merging utilities.
Lists model IDs and related skills for common video workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Uses remote services, so prompts and media URLs may leave the local environment.
Does not guarantee model availability, pricing, speed, or output quality.
Examples require users to provide valid accessible media URLs.

use_cases.title: Create Social Clips
use_cases.description: Generate short concept videos from written prompts for campaign tests or recurring content calendars.

use_cases.title: Animate Product Visuals
use_cases.description: Turn product images into simple motion clips for ads, landing pages, or launch previews.

use_cases.title: Build Avatar Videos
use_cases.description: Prepare talking-head and lipsync workflows using image and audio inputs for instructional material.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a First Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an inference.sh command for a five-second video of a calm city sunrise. Use a fast model and explain each input.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an image-to-video command for this image URL: [image URL]. Add gentle camera motion and note privacy considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Talking Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a lipsync workflow using this portrait URL and audio URL. Recommend a suitable model and list required inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pipeline that generates a clip, adds foley sound, upscales it, and merges it with a second clip. Include review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: User asks for a short product teaser from a text prompt.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a text-to-video model, drafts the command structure, and explains which prompt, duration, and output options to verify.

output_examples.input: User provides an image URL and asks to animate it.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects an image-to-video model, describes required media access, and highlights privacy checks before running the command.

output_examples.input: User wants to polish a silent generated clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines foley, upscaling, and merge steps so the user can build a finished video workflow.

best_practices: Review every generated command before execution, especially prompt text and media URLs.
Use non-confidential test media until provider terms and data handling are understood.
Track model, prompt, duration, and input URLs for reproducible video results.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private media URLs into remote model commands without approval.
Do not run copied installation commands for related skills without separate review.
Do not assume each model supports the same duration, audio, or resolution options.

faq.question: Does this skill generate video locally?
faq.answer: No. It guides inference.sh CLI workflows that use remote model apps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What kinds of video workflows are covered?
faq.answer: It covers text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar video, lipsync, upscaling, foley, and video merging.

faq.question: Can I use private images or audio?
faq.answer: Use caution. Media URLs may be sent to remote services and should follow your privacy requirements.

faq.question: Are the listed models guaranteed to be available?
faq.answer: No. Availability, pricing, speed, and supported inputs can change outside this skill.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it relies on external commands and remote processing of user media.
